Start Your Project
Case studies

Projects that prove how I engineer.

These are not gallery pieces. They are projects where architecture, data flow, and trust matter more than cosmetic novelty. I show the topology and the tradeoffs without exposing sensitive business logic.

Trading infrastructure

SMC SuperFIB

A cross-runtime execution and monitoring system that keeps indicator logic, API state, trading clients, and dashboard visibility aligned.

The hard part was keeping multiple runtimes in agreement without the operator needing to read raw logs to understand what was happening.

Read the full SuperFIB case study

The architecture keeps the API authoritative while the execution and dashboard layers stay readable for operators.

Architecture

The API is the authority. The indicator, execution layer, and dashboard all reconcile against it so parity can be checked and stale state can be caught early.

The value here is not the number of moving parts. It is that each part has a clear role and the operator can see what the system believes without guessing.

Community finance

Stokvel Society

A fintech platform for contribution tracking, ledger visibility, and payout flows designed around clarity for members and administrators.

The hard part was turning a trust-heavy community finance workflow into software that feels calm, clear, and dependable.

Read the full Stokvel Society case study

The product focuses on keeping money movement understandable, auditable, and easy to verify.

Architecture

This flow is intentionally simpler. Contributions move into a ledger layer that acts as the financial core, and the payout path stays understandable to the people relying on it.

The architecture matters because clarity is part of the product. People need to understand where the money is, what changed, and what happens next.

SS Developers
